eraMap.title: The 630d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: First-gen N57 3.0L straight-six; 245 hp, 540 Nm; rear-mounted timing chain is the critical weakness
Revised N57 with 258 hp, 560 Nm; improved timing chain design; EGR/DPF issues remain
Modular B57 engine; 265 hp, 620 Nm; chain relocated; DPF and EGR are the primary maintenance watch items

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 630d? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-LCI F06/F12/F13 (2014+) with N57TU (improved timing chain design)
* G32 (2017+) with the B57 engine (modular design, improved reliability)
* Documented timing chain history on any N57-engined car
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain and bearing longevity)
* Evidence of regular longer runs (DPF health)
avoidIf: * Pre-2014 N57 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N57 is unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF and EGR issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss or oil consumption — may indicate deeper issues
* Any 630d with a history of spun bearings — this is catastrophic

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 630d is BMW's 3.0-litre straight-six diesel flagship across the 6 Series range — offering effortless performance, refinement and economy in a luxury grand tourer package. The N57 (F06/F12/F13) is a powerful and efficient engine with one critical weakness — the rear-mounted timing chain. Post-2014 N57TU versions are improved, but chain maintenance remains important. The B57 (G32 Gran Turismo) is the most refined and reliable, with the chain design improved. All 630d engines share DPF and EGR maintenance requirements. With typical 6 Series values ranging from £10,000 (F13) to £35,000+ (G32), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B57-engined G32 is the safest choice, but a well-maintained N57TU can also serve well.

cta.label: → Compare 630d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 6 Series 630d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling from the rear of the engine (N57 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (N57D30O0, N57D30O1 or B57D30A) and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain — For N57 (F06/F12/F13): check for any chain work or cold-start rattle
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* EGR System — Look for signs of carbon buildup; any performance issues may indicate EGR problems
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Oil Condition — Check for any metallic particles (bearing wear warning)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate DPF, EGR or timing problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 6 Series 630d engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned 630d engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, with the B57 variant at the higher end. Fitting adds £900–£1,500 depending on the specialist. A blown engine replacement on a BMW can cost between £9,000 and £13,000. Reconditioned N57 units are available with 12-month warranty and supply & fit service. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 6 Series.

items.question: Is the BMW 6 Series 630d engine reliable?
items.answer: It depends on which generation you have. The pre-2014 N57 (F06/F12/F13) has a well-documented timing chain stretch risk — the chain is located at the rear of the engine, making replacement expensive. The post-2014 N57TU is improved. The B57 (G32, 2017+) is the most refined and reliable, with the chain design improved. All 630d engines share DPF and EGR maintenance requirements.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 6 Series 630d?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on early N57 engines (pre-2014) — rattling from the rear of the engine is the warning sign. DPF clogging — particularly with short journeys. EGR valve clogging with soot and carbon deposits. Turbocharger wear on high-mileage vehicles. Spun connecting rod bearings — often due to a lack of oil changes.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a 630d engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G32 worth £20,000–£35,000, a £5,500–£7,500 recon is 15–35% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired F13 worth £8,000–£12,000, the same recon may approach our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. B57-engined cars command a premium due to improved reliability.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 6 Series 630d engine code?
items.answer: F06/F12/F13 (2010–2014): N57D30O0. F06/F12/F13 LCI (2014–2018): N57D30O1. G32 Gran Turismo (2017–2024): B57D30A / B57D30O0. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

items.question: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: The chain and guides must be replaced if stretched or worn. A preventative replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 (engine-out job). A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £4,500–£7,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
